description: Tests comprehension of the essay on Books as Companion, including main arguments, benefits of books, and warnings about poor-quality reading material.

questions.question: According to the essay, why are human friendships often unreliable in modern times?
options: They are based on self-interest and break when personal gain conflicts with loyalty
People are too busy with work to maintain friendships
Human beings prefer to read books instead of socializing
Modern technology makes face-to-face friendships impossible
questions.correctAnswer: They are based on self-interest and break when personal gain conflicts with loyalty
questions.explanation: The essay explicitly states that human friendships are based on self-interest and tend to break when there is a conflict between personal gain and loyalty, which is why books serve as better companions.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a benefit that good books provide?
options: They increase wealth and financial prosperity
They sharpen intellectual taste and broaden outlook on life
They console us when we are sad or depressed
They remove ignorance and increase our knowledge
questions.correctAnswer: They increase wealth and financial prosperity
questions.explanation: The essay lists many benefits of books including intellectual improvement, emotional support, and knowledge gain, but does not claim that books increase wealth or financial prosperity.

questions.question: What does the essay identify as the primary dangers of bad books?
options: They are too expensive and difficult to obtain
They can ruin character, develop bad habits, mislead readers, and waste time and energy
They are written in languages that are difficult to understand
They discourage people from developing reading habits
questions.correctAnswer: They can ruin character, develop bad habits, mislead readers, and waste time and energy
questions.explanation: The essay specifically warns that bad books make life miserable, develop bad habits, mislead readers (especially young ones), and waste time and energy, which is why selective reading is important.

questions.question: According to the essay, what makes the pleasure gained from reading books unique compared to other sources of entertainment?
options: Books are the cheapest form of entertainment available
Reading allows us to forget our worries and be transported to a world of beauty, imagination, and happiness, providing deeply fulfilling joy
Books can be shared with large groups of people at once
Reading books requires no effort or concentration from the reader
questions.correctAnswer: Reading allows us to forget our worries and be transported to a world of beauty, imagination, and happiness, providing deeply fulfilling joy
questions.explanation: The essay contrasts the unique pleasure of reading—where we are transported to worlds of beauty and imagination and experience deep fulfillment—with other forms of entertainment like sports, games, and films.

questions.question: Good books serve as both a friend and a philosopher or guide to readers.
options: True
False
questions.explanation: The essay explicitly states in Paragraph 4 that 'A good book is not just a friend. It is also a philosopher and a guide,' emphasizing the dual role books play in a reader's life.

questions.question: The essay recommends that all books, regardless of quality, should be read to develop a healthy reading habit.
options: True
False
questions.explanation: The essay explicitly warns against bad books and emphasizes that students must be careful and selective, reading only good books and avoiding cheap and low-quality books, contradicting the idea that all books should be read.
